issue_title: Add an (approximate) expiry time for names
issue_body:
@vsund commented on [Mon Jan 16 2017](https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-cli/issues/189)

I did a `blockstack whois vsund.id` to see when I have to renew my name, but there are only blocks for creation and expiry

An (approximate) expire in time would be helpful to have a better understanding for when a renewal is necessary.

---
Related: https://forum.blockstack.org/t/will-you-remove-users-who-are-name-squatting/469/4

My understanding is that APIv1 endpoints are pretty much decided (in offline discussions). I'm documenting the current state here.

@jcnelson said on Slack 

```
endpoints are:
* registering a name: `POST /api/v1/names` with JSON payload `{'name': '...'}`
* transferring a name to an address:  `PUT /api/v1/names/:name/owner` with JSON payload `{'owner': '...'}`
* getting the state of all pending transactions:  `GET /api/v1/blockchain/bitcoin/pending`
* getting all names owned by an address: `GET /api/v1//addresses/:address/names`
* getting the address of a name, as well as its zonefile: `GET /api/v1/names/:name`
* getting the address of the owner and wallet of the blockstack node: `GET /api/v1/node/wallet/public
```

I'd also like to identify the **must-haves** from **nice-to-have**, so we can focus on the import endpoints first.

issue_title: systemd service never starts with Type=notify
issue_body:
The documentation [recommends](https://sshuttle.readthedocs.io/en/stable/requirements.html#additional-suggested-software) using the `notify` mode if using a systemd service.

However in that case systemd waits for shuttle to send the notification which never happens and the service is killed because of the start timeout.

Using `Type=simple` in the service file fixes it.

I'm using the [official package on Arch Linux](https://www.archlinux.org/packages/community/any/sshuttle/).
Ironically, I am the one [who asked the Arch package maintainer](https://bugs.archlinux.org/task/56086) to implement the official `Type=notify` recommendation.

Hi we are getting a strange behavior with ASP.net core 2. We have created a simple web API that returns a large amount of JSON data (3.7MB) read from a static JSON file asynchronously. We have found that when we apply load on this application hosted in Azure web apps (> 50 requests) some of the responses contains JSON that is truncated. i.e. we get a status of 200 however the JSON is malformed and truncated. Our original hypothesis we thought was due to the size of the data and and have implemented compression to dis/prove this, however this issue still persists. We have found that this issue does not occur when the size of the Json is less than 1.5 MB and can handle requests > 250.We have also found that when the server resources are increased, i.e. we scale up or out on Azure (from basic) it reduces the frequency it occurs but does not remove it altogether.

To add to the above, we have carried out a similar exercise using ASP.net MVC and we do not get this behavior. The MVC application was also hosted on the same web app.

issue_title: Should not select domains which can't be used with letsencrypt
issue_body:
Neither international (names starting with `xn--`) nor wildcard domains are currently supported by letsencrypt. In the "Which names would you like to activate HTTPS for?" dialog these domains should not be selectable.

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/21212/11580376/fa15c5d2-9a8a-11e5-9be5-379beeeff21d.png)

letsencrypt actually requires datetime >= 1.2, however the dependency in setup.py is unversioned, meaning it can be satisfied by old distro packages. Versioning it forces an install from a newer upstream version, which resolves the breakage.

issue_title: Infinite loop in rrule.rrule if count is set to 0
issue_body:
`rrule.rrule` yields values indefinitely if the `count` argument is set to 0. Example:

```
In [2]: for x in rrule.rrule(freq=rrule.DAILY, count=1): print(x)
2016-12-01 17:53:11

In [3]: for x in rrule.rrule(freq=rrule.DAILY, count=0): print(x)
2016-12-01 17:53:15
2016-12-02 17:53:15
2016-12-03 17:53:15
...
```

Even if 0 is not an acceptable value for that parameter, this looks like a bug.

### What went wrong
`client.is_user_authorized()` opens additional TLS connection to 163.172.167.189:443 and sends some data.

### What I've done
Removed `username.session` file, started capture in wieshark and run client:

```python
from telethon import TelegramClient

API_ID = 12345
API_HASH = '<hash>'

client = TelegramClient('username', API_ID, API_HASH)
client.connect()
print(client.is_user_authorized())
```

### More information
Part of wireshark dump:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1857617/30802221-72735c30-a1ee-11e7-83e4-e470d7af1671.png)

If acces to 163.172.167.189 is blocked (for example with `iptables -A OUTPUT -d 163.172.167.189 -j DROP`) then `is_user_authorized` hangs for ~1 minute but returns without error.

issue_title: Send confirmation email to user after submitting event
issue_body:
After submitting an event via the public form, the user should receive an automatic confirmation email:

```
Thanks for submitting your event to The Ubyssey! 

AMS Events Presents: The 33rd Annual Welcome Back BBQ

You will receive a confirmation email once your event has been approved by our staff. In the meantime, please don't hesitate to contact us if you have any questions.

The Ubyssey Events Team
events@ubyssey.ca
```

Then, after their event has been **approved and published**, they should received another email:

```
Your event has been approved!

You can view your event here: https://www.ubyssey.ca/events/126123

Thanks again for submitting!

The Ubyssey Events Team
events@ubyssey.ca
```

The email messages can be html templates stored in `templates/events/email/success.html` and `templates/events/email/confirm.html`. To render the mail template, use [render_to_string](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.11/topics/templates/#django.template.loader.render_to_string).

Here's an example of how to send an email with Django:

https://github.com/ubyssey/ubyssey-dispatch-theme/blob/develop/ubyssey/views/advertise.py#L19-L25

Then in your `settings.py` file:
```
EMAIL_HOST = 'smtp.gmail.com'
EMAIL_PORT = 465
EMAIL_HOST_USER = 'noreply@ubyssey.ca'
EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD = 'xxxx'
EMAIL_USE_SSL = True
```

EDIT: Include a disapproval email, including a separate landing for the reason (include drop-down with common problems like 'Spam').

issue_title: Improve senza create command line parameters handling
issue_body:
Surprisingly, I couldn't find this being brought up before, so here goes.

Senza create doesn't check for extra parameters on the command line.  Nor can it help you if you misplaced a parameter.  And you have to put them in the certain order, dictated by the .yaml file.

Imagine a .yaml where two numeric parameters follow one another: you run

    senza create my.yaml bla-bla-bla 3 60 <image> ...

but you have misplaced the params, and instead of 3 instances with some check timeout of 60 seconds you are now creating 60 instances with 3 seconds timeout!  So to avoid that you have to keep the .yaml file before your eyes in order to check the order of the parameters.

Wouldn't it be more convenient to provide parameters as key=value?  You can then specify them in any order and the command line becomes self-documenting, e.g.:

    senza create my.yaml APPID=bla-bla-bla NODES=3 TIMEOUT=60 IMAGE=<image> ...

I think for backwards compatibility, we could also keep the current positional approach and support a mix of them (like the Python itself does by the way).

Cheers!

Hi,

kind of "feature request": It would be nice to see the (current) InterOp file format description for each of the bin files here as well (without compiling the sources). 

E.g.
```
    # Quality Metrics (QualityMetricsOut.bin)
    # Contains quality score distribution
    # Format, version 5 (NxSeq500 and newer):
    #  byte 0: file version number (5)
    #  byte 1: length of each record
    #  byte 2: quality score binning (byte flag representing if binning was on)
    #  byte 3: number of quality score bins, B
    #  bytes 4 - (4+B-1): lower boundary of quality score bins
    #  bytes (4+B) - (4+2*B-1): upper boundary of quality score bins
    #  bytes (4+2*B) - (4+3*B-1): remapped scores of quality score bins
    #
    #  The remaining bytes are for the records, with each record in this format:
    #   2 bytes: lane number  (uint16)
    #   2 bytes: tile number  (uint16)
    #   2 bytes: cycle number (uint16)
    #   4 x 50 bytes: number of clusters assigned score (uint32) Q1 through Q50
    #   Where N is the record index
```
That makes it pretty complete. And I don't have to search in almost forgotten PDF files for the descriptions ;-)

best,
Sven

issue_title: "TypeError: <amount> <currency> is not JSON serializable" when serializing a calculated model fields in DRF
issue_body:
I have model which has a calculated MoneyField:

```
class Account(models.Model):
    deposits = MoneyField(decimal_places=2, max_digits=14, default=Money(0, 'USD'))
    withdrawals = MoneyField(decimal_places=2, max_digits=14, default=Money(0, 'USD'))

    @property
    def net_deposits(self):
        return Money(self.deposits.amount - self.withdrawals.amount, self.deposits.currency)
```

My serializer looks like this 

```
class AccountSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    net_deposits = serializers.ReadOnlyField()

    class Meta:
        model = Account
        fields = ('deposits', 'withdrawals', 'net_deposits')
```
 Whenever I try to query a model through this serializer I get the error mentioned in the title, for example:
`TypeError: 80 USD is not JSON serializable`

This is not an issue when the field is directly on the model (not a calculated field).

I've also tried making it a `SerializerMethodField` and return `net_deposit` from the function and am getting the same thing. What's the correct way to get this to work?

issue_title: metadata info not set up in the cell
issue_body:
I was going through the example "Displaying Plots and Images Saved by Other Notebooks". However when I tried to display plots in another notebook, it failed. 
I found that, in the ceil that has the plot, the ceill has the keys:[u'output_type', u'data', u'metadata'], and the data field actuallly has the plot, but the metadata is a empty dictionary. It seems to me the metadata is not correctly set up for the ceil, but I am not sure what happened.
My ipython version is 5.3.0, python is 2.7. matplotlib version is 1.5.1.
![c1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3451083/35123698-5a420854-fc71-11e7-9044-368c4ae16c59.JPG)


![c2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3451083/35123700-5cd3a2a8-fc71-11e7-9f08-7c04a0fed8f4.JPG)

I see all info in the documentation but i haven't idea what's the problem.
![ccct - -run-media-salahaddin-data-proyectos-trabajo-ccct - -ccct-ccct-settings-base py - pycharm 5 0 3_025](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/5034215/12370423/64337760-bbde-11e5-9de5-df983ff0733b.png)

'es' are in settings.LANGUAGE, so, what's the problem?

After running a CFG analysis on a PPC64 ELF binary with symbols, all program-specific functions are missing the symbol-defined name.

This can be replicated by creating an angr project, running either CFGFast or CFGAccurate, and then looking at the functions listed in the knowledge base's function manager.

ELF binaries for other architectures I've tried don't have this problem, including: x86, x64, and ppc32.

issue_title: Play all does not play music on other pages
issue_body: If we are in a playlist consisting of multiple pages. The command `all` or `*` plays all the songs from the current page and ignores songs on the pages. There should be way to play every song in the playlist irrespective of current active page number.

issue_title: Remaining xarray issues in the develop branch
issue_body:
#### Problem description

The `feature-xarray` branch was merged to the `develop` branch recently. This was to reduce the amount of work needed to keep the xarray branch up to date with on-going fixes and features being added to SatPy. **However**, not everything in the xarray branch works or has been converted to work with xarray. This issue is a list of all of the things that still need to be converted or things that do not work in the xarray branch. If something does not work in both the `maint/0.8` branch **and** it does not work in the `develop` branch then it should get its own issue. If you are a maintainer of SatPy just add your items to the lists below, otherwise please comment on this issue and we will add them to the list.

- [ ] Composites
  - [ ] VIIRS adaptive_dnb
  - [ ] VIIRS histogram_dnb
  - [ ] VIIRS dynamic_dnb
  - [ ] VIIRS hncc_dnb
  - [ ] VIIRS true color and false color
- [ ] Documentation
  - [ ] Make sure any mention of `.info` is changed to `.attrs`
  - [ ] Mention that xarray and ask are being used

Untested:
 - [ ] SCMI writer
 - [ ] NinJoTIFF writer
 - [ ] Custom datasets `scn['x'] = xarray.DataArray(my_data, attrs={...})`

issue_title: `resolution` keyword to Scene.load not having desired effect
issue_body:
When loading a composite with the `resolution` keyword set, the resolution chosen is not always respected. This behaviour was shown by running on modis data in both python 2.7 and 3.6. In 2.7, the data loaded was 1000m in 3.6 it was the maximum resolution available.

Here is the test script:
```python
    scn = Scene(platform_name="Aqua", sensor="modis",
                base_dir="/home/a001673/data/satellite",
                reader='hdfeos_l1b',
                start_time=datetime(2016, 8, 5, 0, 0),
                end_time=datetime(2016, 8, 15, 0, 0))

    scn.load(['true_color'], resolution=1000)
```

Hi Martin,

I am developing an application to predict pass conflict of NOAA 19 and NOAA 18 blind orbits with METOP-A and METOP-B passes over EUMETSAT Svalbard ground station. I have been used pyephem as TLE propagator and some days ago I found out also this nice library: pyorbital. I checked the prediction results of both libraries (pyephem and pyorbital) against the data given by the EUMETSAT flight dynamics team and results from pyorbital seem more accurate. Then I would like to integrate also pyorbital in my tool but I found some unexpected results using the get_next_passes function.

Here is the code for verification:
```
"""Pyorbital verification"""

from pyorbital import orbital
from datetime import datetime

def tle_propagator(line1, line2, line3, gs_info, start, stop):
    """Propagates TLEs using pyorbital package"""
    
    dt_format = '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S'
    length = int((stop - start).days*24)
    gs_name = gs_info[0]
    lat = gs_info[1]
    lon = gs_info[2]
    alt = gs_info[3]/1000 # assuming that altitude must be given in km, am I right?
    
    orbit = orbital.Orbital(line1 ,line1 = line2, line2 = line3)
    visibility = orbit.get_next_passes(start,length,lon,lat,alt)

    rise_time = []
    rise_az = []
    max_el_time = []
    max_el = []
    set_time = []
    set_az = []
    duration = []
    sid = []
    antenna = []
    number = []

    for vis in visibility:
        rise_time.append(vis[0].strftime(dt_format))
        rise_az.append(orbit.get_observer_look(vis[0],lon,lat,alt)[0])
        max_el_time.append(vis[2].strftime(dt_format))
        max_el.append(orbit.get_observer_look(vis[2],lon,lat,alt)[1])
        set_time.append(vis[1].strftime(dt_format))
        set_az.append(orbit.get_observer_look(vis[1],lon,lat,alt)[0])
        duration.append((vis[1]-vis[0]).total_seconds())
        number.append(orbit.get_orbit_number(vis[0], tbus_style=True))
        sid.append(line1)
        antenna.append(gs_name)

    results =  [antenna, sid, rise_time, rise_az, max_el_time, max_el,
                set_time, set_az, duration, number]

    return results

if __name__ == '__main__':

    sat = 'METOP-A'
    # TLE from Celestrack updated at 18/08/2015
    if sat == 'METOP-B':         
        line1 = '1 38771U 12049A   15229.80404600  .00000046  00000-0  40891-4 0  9999'
        line2 = '2 38771  98.6922 288.8157 0001608  64.8585  28.9446 14.21489933151176'
    elif sat == 'METOP-A':         
        line1 = '1 29499U 06044A   15229.56308789  .00000049  00000-0  42323-4 0  9993'
        line2 = '2 29499  98.6704 287.8437 0001086  58.6288  78.6540 14.21498242458004'
    elif sat == 'NOAA 19':
        line1 = '1 33591U 09005A   15229.46280543  .00000108  00000-0  83803-4 0  9991'
        line2 = '2 33591  98.9943 179.5032 0014180   2.1481 357.9748 14.11967048336132'
        
    start = datetime(2015,8,20,0,0,0)
    stop = datetime(2015,9,10,0,0,0)
    gs_selected = 'CDA1'
    # ground stations
    if gs_selected == 'CDA1':
        lat = 78.228981740
        lon = 15.388229269
        alt = 490.819 # in m
    elif gs_selected == 'FAIRBANKS':
        lat = 64.973760002
        lon = -147.505823340
        alt = 397.033 # in m
    elif gs_selected == 'WALLOPS':
        lat = 37.947340276
        lon = -75.462919721
        alt = -21.250 # in m
        
    gs = gs_selected, lat, lon, alt
    
    results = tle_propagator(sat,line1,line2,gs,start,stop)
    passes = []
    for row in zip(*results):
        passes.append(row)
    print passes
```

If you run this code you will note that one pass has the following information:
```
('CDA1', 'METOP-A', '2015-09-01 03:04:50', 55.468140794607727, '2015-09-01 03:50:44', -79.594594264864824, '2015-09-01 04:48:11', 88.336125650096918, 6200.204852, 46008)
```
which is clearly wrong, but if you then change the start to datetime(2015,9,1,0,0,0) and stop to datetime(2015,9,2,0,0,0) then you get the following correct results;

I tried all the USB port in my computer , also tried a self-powered usb hub, but it also didn't work.

I recently updated librealsense to the 2.7.9 alpha release in [Fedora Rawhide](https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/librealsense). The package builds fine, but it generates a bogus dependency in the pkg-config file `realsense2.pc`:

```
prefix=/usr
exec_prefix=${prefix}
includedir=${prefix}/include
#TODO: libdir=${exec_prefix}/lib
libdir= ${prefix}/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu

Name:
Description: Intel(R) RealSense(tm) Cross Platform API
Version: 2.7.9
URL: https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense
Requires.private:  Threads libusb-1.0
Libs: -L${libdir} -lrealsense2
Libs.private: 
Cflags: -I${includedir}

#TODO check -Wl -Bdynamic
#Libs: -L${libdir} -Wl,-Bdynamic -lrealsense
```

The problem is with this line:

```
Requires.private:  Threads libusb-1.0
```

At least in Fedora, there is no `Threads.pc`, i.e., no package provides Threads. Thus, using (and in fact installing) the package is currently not possible, because of the unresolved pkg-config requirement.

I don't know about other distributions, so I'm not sure what to suggest. Is there a Threads.pc in Debian/Ubuntu? I don't have access to any Debian-based system, so I can't check, but I would expect the dependency to be broken on all distributions. If that's the case, removing [line 458 in CMakeLists.txt](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/development/CMakeLists.txt#L458) should fix the problem.

Otherwise, can we introduce a case distinction or check if something provides Threads.pc before adding the dependency? I'd like to avoid a downstream patch.

issue_title: Modify the TensorFlow Scheduler and Runtime to Change the Operations Priority
issue_body:
Hi,

I am trying to modify the TensorFlow scheduler and runtime to change the operation priorities. As my understanding, TensorFlow has inter-operation and intra-operation thread pool with a scheduler scheduling operations for different threads and there is also a FIFO queue of operations for operations waiting. The workflow between them is that operations are sent to the inter-op thread pool from the executor, and then that work is running through XLA compiler to eventually be executed on the intra-op thread pool. Schedule() is in inside a specific ThreadPool and is to schedule function for the threads in the pool. The thread scheduler selects a subset of threads to run at any given moment. When the tasks are passed to the ThreadPool, they are added to one of the scheduler thread’s FIFO queues and then the scheduler will pick up the tasks distributed to the available worker threads. I have all the control and data dependencies got from the Graph in TensorFlow by using TensorBoard. Now I am not sure whether my understanding for the TensorFlow scheduler and runtime is correct or not.

The problem is that I still have no clue how and where to modify the threadpool/scheduler/ready_queue or others to change the operations priority/sequence for different threads at the TensorFlow runtime by modifying the source code. Does anyone have any ideas?

issue_title: TensorBoard doesn't respect drive names on Windows
issue_body:
Migrated from https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/issues/7856

@enpasos 

> OS: Windows 10 (64 bit)
> TensorFlow Version: 1.0.0
> CUDA: 8.0
> GPU: yes
> 
> Problem: 
> **C**:>tensorboard --logdir=**E**:\tmp\tensorflow\mnist\logs
> => tensorboard starts without loading data (not working and difficult to detect reason)
> 
> **E**:>tensorboard --logdir=**E**:\tmp\tensorflow\mnist\logs
> => tensorboard starts with loading data (works perfectly) 

@mrry

> Unfortunately TensorBoard [uses a colon](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/27a98083a6c16f263d668271889863596efbeb84/tensorflow/tensorboard/backend/application.py#L581) as the separator between the optional "run name" and the path in the `--logdir` flag. That means that `--logdir=E:\tmp\tensorflow\mnist\logs` is interpreted as a run named `E` with path `\tmp\tensorflow\mnist\logs`, which explains why it's sensitive to the current working directory.
> 
> As a quick workaround, you could avoid this problem by always specifying an explicit run name as part of the `--logdir` flag, e.g.:
> 
> ```
> tensorboard --logdir=training:E:\tmp\tensorflow\mnist\logs
> ```
> 
> (Reassigning to @dandelionmane, who shows up on the blame for the relevant code.)

issue_title: problem reading in orientation data from SUFAR4
issue_body:
There seems to be an issue with how the orientation data are read in in the SUFAR4_asc_magic program in ipmag.py.  The program assumes (I believe) that the azi/dip are actually azimuth and hade field measurements in the SIO convention.  SUFAR4 allows for a more generic approach to orientation and uses four orientation parameters (P1, P2, P3, P4) to describe what azimuth and dip actually mean.  The following modifications generalize the orientations and at least work for the system we are using,
where P1 = 12, P2 = 90, P3 = 12, P4 = 90.  A diagram of the orientation parameters is attached below.

Sorry -- I haven't figured out the whole push request thing yet...

Within:  def SUFAR4_magic...  (ipmag.py)

        if 'Azi' in words and isspec=='0':
            #SampRec['sample_azimuth']=words[1]
            #labaz=float(words[1])
            az = float(words[1])
            P1 = float(words[4])
            P2 = float(words[5]) 
            P3 = float(words[6]) 
            P4 = float(words[7])  # P4 relates to a fabric or bedding measurement -- not dealt with here
            az = az+P1*360./12. - P3*360./12.
            if az>=360:
            	az=az-360
            elif az<=-360:
            	az=az+360
            labaz = az
            SampRec['sample_azimuth']=str(round(az,1))
                            
        if 'Dip' in words:            
            #SampRec['sample_dip']='%7.1f'%(-float(words[1]))
            #labdip=float(words[1])
            SpecRec['specimen_vol']='%8.3e'%(float(words[10])*1e-6) # convert actual volume to m^3 from cm^3
            dip=float(words[1])        
            if P2==90:
        	    dip=dip-90.
            labdip=dip
            SampRec['sample_dip']=str(round(dip,1))

![agico_orientation](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/25388016/23106850/5b607216-f6b9-11e6-8486-9f3a4e11c421.png)

issue_title: Issues with get_paste_buffer() and write_paste_buffer() on macOS
issue_body:
The ``get_paste_buffer`` function doesn't work at all on macOS.  The ``write_paste_buffer`` function works on macOS with Python 3, but doesn't work with Python 2.7.  This means that redirecting output to the paste buffer or taking input from the paste buffer will have issues.

The fix for ``get_paste_buffer`` should be pretty simple, just use the **pbpaste** command-line function instead of **pbcopy**.  

The Python 2 vs Python 3 issues will just require an if/else because the stdout from the subprocess pipe returns a byte string in both Python 2 and Python 3.

issue_title: How to deal with structured/record arrays?

issue_body:
In the pybind11 `detail` code, we have 3 different types of type information:

1. C++ RTTI: `std::type_info`
2. Python's `PyTypeObject`
3. pybind11's extended type information: `detail::type_info`

The variable naming schemes for these 3 kinds of type info objects isn't currently very consistent. `type`, `tinfo`, `type_info`, etc. are used interchangeably for all 3 kinds of type info, which can cause confusion (at least it does for me). And as I've recently found in #1014, it can result in some unfortunate expressions like `x.type->type` (where the second type isn't a Python-style metatype, it's just a different kind of type object).

I'd like to propose a naming convention for these objects (especially when they are members):

1. `std::type_info`: variables named `cpptype` (already in use, but only partially).
2. `PyTypeObject`: `pytype`.
3. `detail::type_info`: it would be nice to rename the type to `detail::extended_type` (to avoid confusion with `std::type_info`) and name variables like `ext_type` or `et`.

What do you think? Does it make sense or am I just bikeshedding?

issue_title: [Bug] Installing scons via 'pip install scons' doesn't work
issue_body:
This is not an Apio bug but is reported here because is affecting the apio installation

This issue is comments in the scons user list:  [installing scons via pip install scons doesn't work](http://scons-users.scons.narkive.com/vNSIhTqN/installing-scons-via-pip-install-scons-doesn-t-work)

When trying to install apio with "sudo pip install apio" there is an strange message gotten:  "error: option --single-version-externally-managed not recognized"

The problem is due to the scons package. It can be reproduced just by installling scons itself with:

```
 sudo pip install scons
```

In windows it seems to be fixed just by updating the setuptools and wheel

```
pip install -U setuptools
pip install -U wheel
```

(but more tests should be done)

The solution in linux is to install scons in a virtual environment. Then, if you install apio (inside or outside the virtualenv) it will work

* Install virtualenv

```
sudo apt install virtualenv python3-virtualenv
```

* Create a virtualenv with python3

```
virtualenv -p python3 test
```

* Enter the virutalenv and activate it:

```bash
cd test
source bin/activate
```

* Install scons

```
pip install scons
```

Now you can install apio inside the virtualenv, or outside

issue_title: tasks in the middle of step process.
issue_body:
Currently a task starts from the `task` keyword and ends at the end of the step. I am wondering if there is a need for something like:

```
input: whatever
task:
   statement1
   statemetn2
task:
   another task
sh:
  a finalizing script.
```

This can currently be achieved with nested workflow, e.g.

```
[task1]
task:
   task1

[task2]
task:
   task2

[default]
input:
sos_run('task1')
sos_run('task2')
```

but I am not sure if the first style is more straightforward.
If we have `for_each={'name': var}`, we should perhaps also have `pair_with={'name': var}`.

Say I have a large number of large raw files stored on a institutional server that are accessible from the cluster. If I would like to process them remotely using sos, I would write a workflow that handles them directly on the cluster...

The problem is that these files do not exist locally so our workflow would choke with 'non-existent' input file etc. 

Perhaps some sort of

```
input: remote('/path/to/file', host=alias)
```
would help? Say,

1. to get signature, sos would login to remote server and calculate it.
2. to process the file locally, sos would copy the file.
3. to process the file on the host, sos would not copy the file.
4. to process the file on another server, sos would copy from host to that server.

Doable but sounds a bit complicated.

issue_title: Managing randomness with faker is impossible
issue_body:
In the case when the fields are specified over faker providers, it's impossible to manage randomness because order of evaluation is not fixed (iterating a dict internally?).
Example:
Seed the random before of instantiation of 
```
class CompanyFactory(factory.DjangoModelFactory):
    class Meta:
        model = Company

    name = faker.company
    status = 'APR'
```
with 

```
import factory
faker = factory.faker.Faker._get_faker(locale='de_DE')
faker.random.seed(0)
```
returns the same result. But adding any other fuzzy field, for instance:
```
faker.phone_number
```
makes both fields to be filled in an unpredictable way. Maybe because the fields are evaluated in the dict iteration.
It would be nice to have a work around at least.

**Background:** Looking into including a subword tokenizer. Been having a difficult time figuring out the right abstractions. The problem is a subword tokenizer unlike other tokenizers requires to define its own subword vocabulary.

Thinking about the above, I have a couple discussion questions.

**Field object vs Batch object abstraction**
The Field object defines instructions for dealing with batches of examples. Should the Batch object handle this responsibility instead? The Field object should only be defined for one example.

**Rename the Field object to TextEncoder**
Tensor2Tensor defines the functionality of converting text to tensors as a "TextEncoder". "TextEncoder" to me is a clearer object name than "Field". Should we rename the "Field" object?
 
Reference: https://github.com/tensorflow/tensor2tensor/blob/master/tensor2tensor/data_generators/text_encoder.py

**Proper abstraction tokenizers and field objects**
The subword tokenizer requires a subword vocabulary. To achieve this, one would need to override the `build_vocab` method. To implement this, you'd need a separate subword field.

This is not consistent with the moses and spacy tokenizers that do not require separate fields.

issue_title: ray create_or_update should restart workers if head node is updated
issue_body:
### Describe the problem
To reproduce:
1. ray create_or_update test.yaml
2. make some change to the head node config
3. ray create_or_update test.yaml

The head node will be restarted since it's config changed, but the workers will not. This is problematic since the workers won't automatically reconnect to the head node, they need to be restarted.

Possible fixes:
- Restart the workers if the head node process gets restarted
- Have workers attempt to reconnect automatically
- Have the head node try to restart worker nodes that are in the cluster but not connected

issue_title: Dropdown widget attributes does not update with options assignment
issue_body:
After the initial creation of a dropdown widget when i  assign a new set of options the view does not update.
I know this issue is the same as #536 that it's already closed, but in my case, the problem came up after a recent update of anaconda, before the code worked well,
I am working on Windows 10
anaconda 5.0.0
jupyter notebook 5.0.0
ipywidgets 7.0.0
traitlets 4.3.2

The code is,

```
# widget Dropdown
import ipywidgets as widgets
from IPython.display import display

OptDict={'XXX':0,'YYY':1}
wDd2=widgets.Dropdown(options=OptDict,value=0,disabled=False)
print(wDd2, id(wDd2),"\n")

with wDd2.hold_trait_notifications():
    OptDict['ZZZ']=9
    wDd2.options=OptDict
    wDd2.value=1
print(wDd2.options, id(wDd2.options))
print(wDd2._options_labels, wDd2._options_values)

display(wDd2)

```
and the output,

```
Dropdown(options={'XXX': 0, 'YYY': 1}, value=0) 2205788730032 

{'ZZZ': 9, 'XXX': 0, 'YYY': 1} 2205789360520
('XXX', 'YYY') (0, 1)
```
It seems that the Dropdown widgets atributes (_options_labels, _options_values) are not updated with the assigment of wDd2.options=OptDict.
Are there any other instructions that force the updating of these attributes? or it is possible to synchronize them automatically with any changes of the variable 'OptDict' assigned at the Dropdown constructor?

issue_title: AWS Lambda - Create lambda function does not return function details
issue_body:
When creating a new lambda function using the CLI, the function is created, but the lambda details are not returned.

## Context

According to the [AWS Lambda API documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/API_CreateFunction.html), creating a new lambda function will return the details of the function created.

## Process

1. Package the lambda into a zip archive.
2. Create a new lambda using AWS CLI command
`aws --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4574 --region=us-east-1 lambda create-function --function-name=myFunction --runtime=nodejs6.10 --role=arn:aws:iam:local --handler=lambda.handler --zip-file=fileb://lambda.zip`
3. The lambda function is created and the result printed on the screen.

## Expected result

```javascript
HTTP/1.1 201
Content-type: application/json

{
   "CodeSha256": "string",
   "CodeSize": number,
   "DeadLetterConfig": { 
      "TargetArn": "string"
   },
   "Description": "string",
   "Environment": { 
      "Error": { 
         "ErrorCode": "string",
         "Message": "string"
      },
      "Variables": { 
         "string" : "string" 
      }
   },
   "FunctionArn": "string",
   "FunctionName": "string",
   "Handler": "string",
   "KMSKeyArn": "string",
   "LastModified": "string",
   "MasterArn": "string",
   "MemorySize": number,
   "Role": "string",
   "Runtime": "string",
   "Timeout": number,
   "TracingConfig": { 
      "Mode": "string"
   },
   "Version": "string",
   "VpcConfig": { 
      "SecurityGroupIds": [ "string" ],
      "SubnetIds": [ "string" ],
      "VpcId": "string"
   }
}
```

## Current result

`{}`

Localstack implementation doesn't respect filtering and always returns all stored objects.

### Amazon S3 setup
```
$ export BUCKET=xyz_abcdef
$ export ENDPOINT=
$ aws $ENDPOINT s3 mb s3://$BUCKET
$ for i in `seq 1 10`; do echo hello$i | aws $ENDPOINT s3 cp - s3://$BUCKET/hello$i; done
$ aws $ENDPOINT s3api list-objects --bucket $BUCKET | jq '.Contents | length'
10
```
### OK
```
$ aws $ENDPOINT s3api list-objects --bucket $BUCKET --max-key 1 | jq '.Contents | length'
1
$ aws $ENDPOINT s3api list-objects --bucket $BUCKET --starting-token hello8 | jq '.Contents | length'
1
```

### Localstack setup
```
$ export BUCKET=xyz_abcdef
$ export ENDPOINT='--endpoint-url http://localhost:4572'
$ aws $ENDPOINT s3 mb s3://$BUCKET
$ for i in `seq 1 10`; do echo hello$i | aws $ENDPOINT s3 cp - s3://$BUCKET/hello$i; done
$ aws $ENDPOINT s3api list-objects --bucket $BUCKET | jq '.Contents | length'
10
```
### FAIL
```
$ aws $ENDPOINT s3api list-objects --bucket $BUCKET --max-key 1 | jq '.Contents | length'
10
$ aws $ENDPOINT s3api list-objects --bucket $BUCKET --starting-token hello8 | jq '.Contents | length'
10
```

Thank you!

issue_title: SQLite store open attempts to re-create existing store
issue_body:
gsf747@gmail.com, 2009-07-14T06:06:06.000Z 
The docstring on the SQLite store open() states, "If create is True a store
will be created if it does not already exist."  However, a call to open
with create=True attempts to create the tables again, leading to the
following error:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "", line 1, in 
  File "rdflib/store/SQLite.py", line 52, in open
    c.execute(CREATE_ASSERTED_STATEMENTS_TABLE%(self._internedId))
pysqlite2.dbapi2.OperationalError: table kb_48181acd22_asserted_statements
